explanation: This LTL property states that globally, if the robot determines that a planned path is not feasible (pathImpossible), it will eventually explain the available alternative courses of action (explainAlternatives). This directly addresses the user's inquiry about the robot's 'fall-backs' and what it would do if a path is blocked, by indicating it would provide information about alternatives.

This LTL property states that globally, if the robot determines that a planned path is not feasible (pathImpossible), it will eventually explain its plan for self-recovery (explainRecoveryStrategy). This directly captures the concept of 'fall-backs' by focusing on the robot's ability and explanation of its self-recovery strategies.

This LTL property states that globally, if the robot determines that a planned path is not feasible (pathImpossible), it will eventually either explain alternative courses of action or explain its self-recovery strategy. This combines the two most direct and informative responses to the 'fall-back' question.

This LTL property states that globally, if the robot determines that a planned path is not feasible (pathImpossible), it will eventually change its plan (changePlan). This captures a direct action the robot would take as a 'fall-back' when facing an impossible path.

This LTL property states that globally, if the robot determines that a planned path is not feasible (pathImpossible), it will eventually change its plan (changePlan) and eventually explain the available alternative courses of action (explainAlternatives). This suggests both an action and a subsequent explanation related to fall-backs.

This LTL property states that globally, if the robot determines that a planned path is not feasible (pathImpossible), it will eventually change its plan (changePlan) and eventually explain its self-recovery strategy (explainRecoveryStrategy). This combines the action of changing plan with the explanation of recovery as a fall-back.

This LTL property states that globally, if the robot determines that a planned path is not feasible (pathImpossible), it will eventually either change its plan, explain alternatives, or explain its self-recovery strategy. This covers a broad range of highly relevant responses to an impossible path.

This LTL property states that globally, if the robot determines that a planned path is not feasible (pathImpossible), it will eventually change its planned route (routeChange). This is a specific action related to adapting to an unfeasible path, serving as a 'fall-back'.

This LTL property states that globally, if the robot determines that a planned path is not feasible (pathImpossible), it will eventually provide a general explanation (explain). This is a broader interpretation of 'what would it do?' if a specific explanation isn't guaranteed.

This LTL property states that globally, if the robot determines that a planned path is not feasible (pathImpossible), it will eventually explain the off-normal condition (explainOffNormal). An impossible path is an off-normal condition, and explaining it would be part of its 'fall-back' information.
